articles.title: Dependable C
articles.summary: Dependable C is an attempt to document a subset of C for developers who want a stable and reliable library with fast execution, suitable for use in high-level languages like Python, Java, and C#. It prioritizes compile-time compilation and minimal dependencies, making it easy to integrate into existing toolchains. The goal is to provide a simple, plain C implementation that can be used by anyone without requiring special bindings or compiler settings, allowing developers to focus on writing reliable code rather than maintaining the library itself.

By leveraging microVMs, developers can streamline their development workflow and focus on building applications rather than managing complex infrastructure.

articles.title: The notification that gave up after a minute
articles.summary: The notification that gave up after a minute is a case study in push notifications, where a server signs a message, hands it to a service provider, and then waits for the browser to wake a service worker before displaying a banner. The key takeaway is that the first dropped baton of silence was due to a site-data sweep while the subscription was still valid, allowing the page to silently call new Notification() without error.
